title: 10 Essential Tips to Save Money on Currency Exchange
author: FastCurrency Team
content:
1. Always Avoid Airport and Hotel Currency Exchanges

Airport kiosks and hotel desks offer some of the worst exchange rates, with commissions as high as 15%. Plan ahead and exchange a small amount at your local bank before traveling, then use local ATMs after arrival.

2. Use Mid-Market Rate Tools Like FastCurrency

Before any transaction, check the real mid-market rate using our live currency converter. This gives you a baseline to compare what banks or exchange services offer.

3. Prefer Local ATMs (But Beware of Dynamic Conversion)

Withdraw local currency from a bank-affiliated ATM. Always choose 'without conversion' (in local currency) when the machine asks — otherwise you pay a terrible hidden markup.

4. Get a No-Foreign-Transaction-Fee Credit Card

Cards like Wise, Revolut, or many premium travel cards eliminate the typical 3% foreign transaction fee. Use them for purchases and ATM withdrawals where possible.

5. Never Exchange More Than You Need

Currency fluctuates and leftover foreign cash often gets exchanged back at a loss. Use a converter to estimate daily expenses and withdraw smaller amounts.

6. Compare Peer-to-Peer Exchange Services

Apps like Wise or CurrencyFair often beat bank rates. They use the real mid-market rate and charge a low transparent fee.

7. Watch Out for 'Zero Commission' Tricks

Some services advertise 0% commission but give you a terrible exchange rate. Always calculate the final amount you'll receive — the spread is where they profit.

8. Time Your Exchange (If Possible)

While long-term prediction is impossible, avoid exchanging when markets are highly volatile (e.g., major political events). Use rate alerts to get a sense of trends.

9. Use Multi-Currency Accounts

If you travel often or work remotely, open a multi-currency account. You can hold and exchange multiple currencies at interbank rates and spend with a linked card.

10. Always Double-Check with a Reliable Converter

Before any exchange, quickly check the real rate on FastCurrency. It takes 2 seconds and can save you from bad deals. Bookmark our tool for your next trip!

Final thought: A few minutes of research can save you 5–10% of your travel money. Combine our currency converter with the tips above, and you'll be a smarter traveler.

title: How to Get the Best EUR to USD Exchange Rate in 2026
author: FastCurrency Team
content:
Understanding the EUR/USD Pair

The Euro to US Dollar (EUR/USD) is the most traded currency pair in the world. Even small fluctuations can mean significant differences when transferring large sums. In 2026, the pair is influenced by central bank policies, inflation data, and geopolitical events.

1. Monitor ECB and Fed Announcements

Interest rate decisions from the European Central Bank (ECB) and the Federal Reserve (Fed) directly impact the exchange rate. Use economic calendars to track rate decision dates and avoid exchanging right before major announcements.

2. Avoid Weekend and After-Hours Trades

Currency markets close on weekends. Rates often gap when trading resumes Sunday evening (EST). For the best transparency, exchange during active market hours (Monday–Friday).

3. Use Limit Orders if Transferring Large Amounts

Many currency specialists allow you to set a target rate (limit order). If EUR/USD hits your desired level, the trade executes automatically. This removes the need to watch screens all day.

4. Compare Banks vs. Online Specialists

Traditional banks add a markup of 3–5% on top of the mid-market rate. Online services like Wise, CurrencyFair, or OFX offer rates much closer to the real market. Always compare using FastCurrency's real-time rates before committing.

5. Beware of 'Zero-Fee' Offers with Bad Rates

Some providers claim 'no transfer fee' but hide profit in the exchange rate. Calculate the final amount you'll receive using our currency converter. If the rate is more than 0.5% from the mid-market rate, you're overpaying.

6. Timing Strategies: Averaging In

If you need to transfer a large sum over several months, consider splitting it into smaller chunks and exchanging at regular intervals (e.g., weekly). This reduces the risk of catching a bad peak.

7. Watch for Seasonal Trends

Historically, the US dollar tends to strengthen in late spring and weaken toward year-end, but past performance doesn't guarantee future results. Use our tool daily to stay informed.

Final Checklist Before Exchanging

  • Check the live mid-market rate on FastCurrency.
  • Compare at least three providers (bank, online specialist, app).
  • Calculate the total cost including any hidden fees.
  • If the amount is large (>€10,000), negotiate with a broker.

By following these steps, you can save hundreds of dollars on a single EUR/USD transfer. Bookmark FastCurrency and make it your first stop before any currency exchange.
